THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR NORMANDY BLVD AND BLAIR ROAD NEAR 30.2727N -81.8308W. DAMAGE IN THIS AREA WAS MINOR EF0 DAMAGE WITH SOME FENCING BLOWN OVER AND TREES DAMAGED. THE VORTEX CONTINUED ALONG BLAIR ROAD MAINTAINING THE SAME INTENSITY UNTIL IT REACHED WOOD DOVE WAY FROM THERE TO INTERSTATE 10 IT EXHIBITED EF1 DAMAGE. THE FUNNEL THEN MOVED THROUGH THE PUBLIX DISTRIBIUTION CENTER FLIPPING EMPTY TRUCKS AND THEN NNE TO ARBEE RD NEAR 30.3285 -81.8201. PATH LENGTH 4 MILES...WIDTH .1 MILE.
NWS STORM SURVEY HAS DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO OCCURRED AROUND THE CRYSTAL SPRINGS ROAD AND BLAIR ROAD AREA. EF SCALE HAS NOT BEEN DETERMINED AT THIS TIME.

AS THE WESTSIDE-CRYSTAL SPRINGS TORNADO WAS DISSIPATING THE THUNDERSTORM REAR FLANK DOWNDRAFT MOVED THROUGH A NEIGHBORHOOD NORTHEAST OF THE DISIPATING FUNNEL. THE DOWNDRAFT CONTACTED THE GROUND BEAR WEST BEAVER STREET AND MOVED NORTHEAST THOUGH THE WATERSHED DRIVE...MAPLE AVE AND ORME ROAD AREA. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1 TO POSSIBLY 1.5 MILES IN LENGTH AND ABOUTH .1 MILE WIDE. THE PREDOMINAT DAMAGE WAS TO TREES WITH SOME ROOF DAMAGE ON MAPLE STREET. START 30.3169 -81.8176 TO 30

EXTENSTIVE FLOODING OF FLETCHER AVENUE OCCURRED, ESPECIALLY NEAR THE 600 BLOCK SECTION WHICH EXTENDED SOUTHWARD TO THE 1500 BLOCK OF FLETCHER AVENUE AND SOUTHWARD TO OCEAN DRIVE. THESE AREAS ARE JUST EAST OF EGANS CREEK. WATER LEVEL OBSERVATIONS INDICATED A SEICHE FLOOD TRAILING THE STORM SURGE THAT LIKELY FLOODED EGANS CREEK AS HURRICANE MATTHEW STARTED TO TRACK FARTHER NORTH OF THE LOCAL AREA WITH A SWITCH IN WINDS TO THE NNW PUSHING SURGE WATERS BACK ACROSS THE EGANS CREEK DRAINAGE.
